content_en:
"1 Related by Bukhaaree from the hadith of Ibn Mas‘ood (5065), Muslim

(1400), Ahmad (3581), At-Tirmidhee (1081), An-Nassaaee (2239), Abu Daawood (2046), Ibn Maajah (1845), and Ad-Daarimee (2165).

Related by Bukhaaree (5063), Muslim (1401), Ahmad (13122), and AnNassaaee (3217).

Chapter - 21 The Manners Of Interacting With One's Wife 453,

In another narration from Anas Ibn Maalik 4, the Messenger of Allah 3 said,

“Made beloved to me from the world are perfume and women; and the prayer has been made the delight of my eye.’"")

Celibacy is not from the way of the Messengers. Imam Ahmad said, “Celibacy has nothing to do with Islam. Whoever invites you to other than marriage, has invited you to other than Islam.” It is compulsory upon a person to marry if he desires to marry, if he is able to marry, and if he fears that he will otherwise succumb to temptation.

The following verse provides the framework for how a man should treat his wife:

“And they (women) have rights (over their husbands as regards living expenses, etc.) similar (to those of their husbands) over

them (as regards obedience and respect, etc.) to what is reasonable”’ (Quran 2:228)

Just as men have rights over their wives, women have rights over their husbands. To know the extent of those rights, people should refer to what is known as Al-Ma’roof, or the general customs and norms in a given society at a given time. How much a man must spend on his wife, how much clothing he has to provide for her, what kind of accommodations he must provide for her, how often he has to have intimate relations with her - all of these depend on a person’s individual situation and, as we said, on the general customs and norms in a given society at a give time - AlMa’roof. This is the ruling for a marriage contract that is void of conditions. If a marriage contract has conditions stipulated in it

"] Related by Ahmad (11884) and An-Nassaaee (3939). Al-Albaanee said, “Hasan sahih.” (3680).

by either the husband or wife, those conditions must be followed, as long as Halal (lawful) is not made Haram (forbidden), and Haram is not made Halal. Ibn ‘Abbaas # said, “Verily, I love to adorn myself (i.e., make myself look good) for my wife, just as I love for her to adorn herself for me, because Allah 3€ says: “And they (women) have rights (over their husbands as regards living expenses, etc.) similar (to those of their husbands) over them (as regards obedience and respect, etc.) to what is reasonable”’ (Quran 2:228)

Mu’aawiyah Ibn Haidah said, “A man asked the Prophet 3%, ‘What right does a woman have over her husband?’ He #¢ said, Ng hgh CEG 81 ky Sb 1 Yaak ‘He should feed her when he eats and clothe her when he clothes himself (i.e., purchase clothes for her); he must not strike her face,

nor must he curse her or say hurtful words to her; and he should not forsake her except in their home."”"4)

Question: In routine domestic affairs - such as preparing meals, cleaning the house, etc. - does a wife have to serve her husband?

Answer: Shaikh Al-Islam Ibn Taymiyyah said, “Scholars disagree: does a woman have to serve her husband to fix up the mattress, to serve food, drink, and bread, to knead dough, to give food to his servants and livestock, and so on? Some scholars have said, ‘She does not have to serve him,’ but this view is weak, weak like view in which it is held that a man does not have to have intimate relations (sexual intercourse) with his wife. If he doesn’t, then he isn’t treating her well... And it is said that - this is the correct view ~she must serve him, for a husband is her chief

"1 Related by Ahmad (19511); by Abu Daawood (2142), and in grading his narration, Al-Albaanee said, “Hasan sahih’’; and by Ibn Maajah (1850).

Chapter - 21 The Manners Of Interacting With One's Wife 455

in Allah’s Book...Among the scholars who hold this view are those who say, ‘She must serve him a little bit,’ while others among them say, ‘She must serve him according to Al-Ma’roof,’ and this latter view is correct. A woman must serve her husband according to Al-Ma’roof, which means that the standard of society based on their status applies to them. The help provided by a wife who lives in the desert is not like the help provided by a wife who is a city-dweller, and the help of a strong woman is not like the help of a weak woman (so the matter depends upon AIMa’roof - upon the situation of the husband and wife, and upon the norms of the society they live in).’”

3) Gentleness In Dealing With Women

The Prophet # ordered men to treat their womenfolk with kindness and gentleness. Women are innately weak, and so they are need of kind and compassionate treatment, and not of the rough behavior that is common among men when they deal with one another. Abu Hurairah reported that the Messenger of

Allah # said, cers | ight Sa te GE ey Lose iL \yo Cn roa

Part of treating women well is to teach them what they need to know from the affairs of their religion - such as the tulings for purity, menstruation, post-natal bleeding, prayer, Zakaat, and so on. If a husband cannot teach her because he doesn’t have

" Related by Bukhaaree (3331), Muslim (1468), Ahmad (9240), At-Tirmidhee (1188), and Ad-Daarimee (2222).

enough knowledge, he must provide her with the resources to learn elsewhere - at the very least to learn those matters of the religion that she must know. A husband can do that in many ways; for example, he can buy religious books or audiocassettes for her, or he can send her to religious study circles.

Another aspect of treating women well is to make sure they

perform their obligatory religious duties — one of them being the wearing of modest clothing and the Hijaab. Allah 3¢ said:

“And enjoin As-salah (the prayer) on your family, and be patient in offering them fi.e., the salah (prayers)]’’ (Qur’an 20:132) And Allah 3 said:

“O you who believe! Ward off from yourselves and your families a Fire (Hell) whose fuel is men and stones” (Quran 66:6)

After Maalik Ibn Al-Huwairith 4 and a delegation that came with him had stayed in Madeenah for twenty days, they decided to return to their homes. The Prophet 2% said to them,

Fo gse oF Bien ae we a yet 4 . Faded 8925 Ales ped LOSE RT UI ars “Return to your families, stay with them, teach them, and order them [to perform their religious duties}...""")

When a women is negligent in performing her religious duties, when she doesn’t wear the proper Islamic Hijaab, when she refuses to go to her husband when he invites her to his bed, or when she disobeys him in a matter that requires her obedience — her husband must discipline her in such a way as to bring her back to uprightness.

There are stages of disciplining that are outlined in the Shariah, and a husband should not skip to an advanced stage until the

Chapter ~ 21 The Manners Of Interacting With One's Wife 457

one before it becomes impossible for him. Allah 3€ said: ard CAs

ob ARG eptaat 3 YG CALS OG GE sth> 1O yoo EE HY Se Se IE SE

“As to those women on whose part you see ill-conduct, admonish them (first), (next), refuse to share their beds, (and last) beat them (lightly, if it is useful), but if they return to obedience, seek not against them means (of annoyance). Surely, Allah is Ever Most High, Most Great).” (Quran 4:34)

The first stage involves admonishing one’s wife. In this stage, one should remind his wife of her religious duties and of Allah’s punishment for disobeying Him. In the second stage, he should stay away from the bed they share. And in the third stage, he should hit her lightly - not to hurt her because he is only allowed to hit her lightly, but to show her the seriousness of the matter - but not with that intensity which one shows when he is giving vent to his anger and rage.

Question: If one has a wife that does not pray, should he order her to pray? And if she still refuses, what should he do?

Answer: Yes, not only may he order her to pray, he must order her to pray. Allah 3¢ said:

CE et lat cI Zh

“And enjoin As-salah (the prayer) on your family, and be patient in offering them [i.e., the salah (prayers)].”” (Quran 20:132)

And Allah 3€ said: C5eLG ZO hE 06 Kah Kah ee galt Ge> “O you who believe! Ward off from yourselves and your families a Fire (Hell) whose fuel is men and stones.”” (Quran 66:6) And the Prophet # said,

565 tle “Teach them and discipline them.”

When one orders his wife to pray, he should do so in an encouraging way, just as he encourages her to do something that he wants her to do for his personal benefit. Then, if a woman persists in refusing to pray, he should divorce her; and according to the correct view in the issue, it is compulsory upon him to divorce her. In general, scholars agree that a person who refuses to pray deserves to be punished until he begins to pray again; and if a person continually refuses to pray — after being punished and warned for a period of time — then he should be killed the death of an apostate. This is one of two scholarly views regarding the issue in question, and Shaikh Al-Islam is among those who hold it. And Allah 4% knows best.

4) Joking And Playing With One’s Wife

Some husbands think it unmanly for them to joke and play with their wives; others feel that, by playing with their wives, they will lose the respect they deserve in their homes. Yet the best, most manly, and most respected of husbands - the Prophet # - would both joke and play with his wives. He 2% said,
content_id:
"1 Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhaaree dari hadith Ibn Mas'ood (5065), Muslim

(1400), Ahmad (3581), At-Tirmidhee (1081), An-Nassaaee (2239), Abu Daawood (2046), Ibn Maajah (1845), dan Ad-Daarimee (2165).

Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhaaree (5063), Muslim (1401), Ahmad (13122), dan AnNassaaee (3217).

Bab - 21 Adab Berinteraksi Dengan Istri 453,

Dalam riwayat lain dari Anas Ibn Maalik, Rasulullah bersabda,

"Dijadikan aku mencintai dari dunia ini: wewangian dan wanita; dan salah dijadikan kesenangan mataku.'"

Membujang bukanlah dari jalan para Rasul. Imam Ahmad berkata, "Membujang tidak ada hubungannya dengan Islam. Barangsiapa mengajakmu kepada selain pernikahan, berarti ia mengajakmu kepada selain Islam." Seseorang wajib menikah apabila ia ingin menikah, mampu untuk menikah, dan khawatir bahwa dirinya akan terjatuh dalam fitnah jika tidak menikah.

Ayat berikut memberikan kerangka tentang bagaimana seorang suami seharusnya memperlakukan istrinya:

"Dan mereka (para wanita) mempunyai hak (atas suami mereka dalam hal nafkah, dll.) yang serupa (dengan hak suami mereka)

atas mereka (dalam hal ketaatan dan penghormatan, dll.) secara ma'ruf" (Quran 2:228)

Sebagaimana suami memiliki hak atas istrinya, para istri pun memiliki hak atas suami mereka. Untuk mengetahui sejauh mana hak-hak tersebut, hendaknya orang merujuk kepada apa yang dikenal sebagai Al-Ma'roof, yaitu adat dan norma umum dalam suatu masyarakat pada suatu masa tertentu. Berapa besar nafkah yang wajib diberikan suami kepada istrinya, berapa banyak pakaian yang harus ia sediakan untuknya, tempat tinggal seperti apa yang harus ia sediakan untuknya, seberapa sering ia harus berhubungan intim dengannya — semua ini bergantung pada situasi masing-masing orang dan, sebagaimana telah disebutkan, pada adat dan norma umum dalam suatu masyarakat pada suatu masa tertentu — Al-Ma'roof. Inilah hukum untuk akad nikah yang tidak disertai syarat-syarat. Apabila suatu akad nikah memiliki syarat-syarat yang ditentukan di dalamnya

"] Diriwayatkan oleh Ahmad (11884) dan An-Nassaaee (3939). Al-Albaanee berkata, "Hasan sahih." (3680).

oleh suami atau istri, maka syarat-syarat tersebut wajib dipenuhi, selama yang halal tidak dijadikan haram, dan yang haram tidak dijadikan halal. Ibn 'Abbaas berkata, "Sesungguhnya aku senang berhias (yakni, membuat diriku tampak baik) untuk istriku, sebagaimana aku senang apabila ia berhias untukku, karena Allah berfirman: "Dan mereka (para wanita) mempunyai hak (atas suami mereka dalam hal nafkah, dll.) yang serupa (dengan hak suami mereka) atas mereka (dalam hal ketaatan dan penghormatan, dll.) secara ma'ruf"' (Quran 2:228)

Mu'aawiyah Ibn Haidah berkata, "Seorang laki-laki bertanya kepada Nabi, 'Hak apakah yang dimiliki seorang wanita atas suaminya?' Beliau bersabda, 'Hendaknya ia memberinya makan ketika ia makan dan memberinya pakaian ketika ia berpakaian (yakni, membelikan pakaian untuknya); ia tidak boleh memukul wajahnya,

dan tidak boleh mencelanya atau mengucapkan kata-kata yang menyakiti hatinya; dan ia tidak boleh menjauhinya kecuali di dalam rumah mereka.'"

Pertanyaan: Dalam urusan rumah tangga sehari-hari — seperti menyiapkan makanan, membersihkan rumah, dan sebagainya — apakah seorang istri wajib melayani suaminya?

Jawaban: Syaikh Al-Islam Ibn Taymiyyah berkata, "Para ulama berbeda pendapat: apakah seorang wanita wajib melayani suaminya untuk membereskan kasur, menghidangkan makanan, minuman, dan roti, menguleni adonan, memberi makan para pembantunya dan ternaknya, dan sebagainya? Sebagian ulama berkata, 'Ia tidak wajib melayaninya,' namun pendapat ini lemah, lemah seperti pendapat yang menyatakan bahwa seorang suami tidak wajib berhubungan intim (bersetubuh) dengan istrinya. Jika ia tidak melakukannya, berarti ia tidak memperlakukannya dengan baik... Dan dikatakan bahwa — inilah pendapat yang benar — ia wajib melayaninya, karena suami adalah pemimpinnya

"1 Diriwayatkan oleh Ahmad (19511); oleh Abu Daawood (2142), dan dalam menilai riwayatnya, Al-Albaanee berkata, "Hasan sahih"; dan oleh Ibn Maajah (1850).

Bab - 21 Adab Berinteraksi Dengan Istri 455

dalam Kitab Allah... Di antara para ulama yang berpendapat demikian, ada yang berkata, 'Ia wajib melayaninya sedikit,' sementara yang lain di antara mereka berkata, 'Ia wajib melayaninya sesuai dengan Al-Ma'roof,' dan pendapat yang terakhir inilah yang benar. Seorang wanita wajib melayani suaminya sesuai dengan Al-Ma'roof, yang berarti bahwa standar masyarakat berdasarkan status mereka berlaku bagi mereka. Bantuan yang diberikan oleh seorang istri yang tinggal di pedesaan tidaklah sama dengan bantuan yang diberikan oleh seorang istri yang tinggal di perkotaan, dan bantuan seorang wanita yang kuat tidaklah sama dengan bantuan seorang wanita yang lemah (maka persoalan ini bergantung pada Al-Ma'roof — pada situasi suami dan istri, serta pada norma masyarakat tempat mereka tinggal).'"

3) Kelembutan Dalam Memperlakukan Wanita

Nabi memerintahkan para laki-laki untuk memperlakukan kaum wanita dengan kebaikan dan kelembutan. Wanita secara fitrah bersifat lemah, sehingga mereka membutuhkan perlakuan yang penuh kebaikan dan kasih sayang, bukan perilaku kasar yang lazim di antara para laki-laki ketika mereka saling berinteraksi. Abu Hurairah meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah bersabda,

Bagian dari memperlakukan wanita dengan baik adalah mengajari mereka apa yang perlu mereka ketahui dari urusan agama mereka — seperti hukum-hukum bersuci, haid, nifas, salah, Zakaat, dan sebagainya. Apabila seorang suami tidak dapat mengajarinya karena ia tidak memiliki

" Diriwayatkan oleh Bukhaaree (3331), Muslim (1468), Ahmad (9240), At-Tirmidhee (1188), dan Ad-Daarimee (2222).

pengetahuan yang cukup, ia wajib menyediakan sarana baginya untuk belajar di tempat lain — setidaknya untuk mempelajari hal-hal agama yang wajib ia ketahui. Seorang suami dapat melakukan itu dengan berbagai cara; misalnya, ia dapat membelikan buku-buku agama atau kaset audio untuknya, atau ia dapat mengizinkannya menghadiri majelis-majelis ilmu agama.

Aspek lain dari memperlakukan wanita dengan baik adalah memastikan mereka

melaksanakan kewajiban-kewajiban agama mereka — salah satunya adalah mengenakan pakaian yang menutup aurat dan Hijaab. Allah berfirman:

"Dan perintahkanlah kepada keluargamu mendirikan salah (shalat), dan bersabarlah kamu dalam mengerjakannya [yakni, salah (shalat-shalat) tersebut]" (Quran 20:132) Dan Allah berfirman:

"Wahai orang-orang yang beriman! Peliharalah dirimu dan keluargamu dari api neraka yang bahan bakarnya adalah manusia dan batu" (Quran 66:6)

Setelah Maalik Ibn Al-Huwairith dan rombongan yang bersamanya menetap di Madeenah selama dua puluh hari, mereka memutuskan untuk kembali ke kampung halaman mereka. Nabi bersabda kepada mereka,

"Kembalilah kepada keluarga kalian, tinggallah bersama mereka, ajarilah mereka, dan perintahkanlah mereka [untuk melaksanakan kewajiban-kewajiban agama mereka]...'"

Ketika seorang wanita lalai dalam melaksanakan kewajiban agamanya, ketika ia tidak mengenakan Hijaab Islam yang semestinya, ketika ia menolak mendatangi suaminya ketika ia mengajaknya ke tempat tidurnya, atau ketika ia mendurhakai suaminya dalam suatu perkara yang mengharuskan ketaatannya — suaminya wajib mendisiplinkannya dengan cara yang dapat mengembalikannya kepada kelurusan.

Terdapat tahapan-tahapan mendisiplinkan yang telah digariskan dalam Syariah, dan seorang suami tidak boleh melompat ke tahap yang lebih lanjut hingga

Bab - 21 Adab Berinteraksi Dengan Istri 457

tahap sebelumnya tidak mungkin lagi dijalankan olehnya. Allah berfirman:

"Wanita-wanita yang kamu khawatirkan nusyuznya, maka nasihatilah mereka (pertama), (kemudian) pisahkanlah mereka di tempat tidur mereka, dan (terakhir) pukullah mereka (dengan ringan, jika bermanfaat), tetapi jika mereka menaatimu, maka janganlah kamu mencari-cari jalan untuk menyusahkan mereka. Sesungguhnya Allah Maha Tinggi lagi Maha Besar." (Quran 4:34)

Tahap pertama melibatkan menasihati istri. Dalam tahap ini, seseorang hendaknya mengingatkan istrinya akan kewajiban agamanya dan akan azab Allah atas kedurhakaan kepada-Nya. Pada tahap kedua, ia hendaknya menjauhi tempat tidur yang mereka bagi bersama. Dan pada tahap ketiga, ia boleh memukulnya dengan ringan — bukan untuk menyakitinya karena ia hanya diperbolehkan memukulnya dengan ringan, tetapi untuk menunjukkan kepadanya keseriusan persoalan tersebut — namun bukan dengan kekerasan yang ditunjukkan seseorang ketika ia melampiaskan amarah dan kemarahannya.

Pertanyaan: Apabila seseorang memiliki istri yang tidak melaksanakan salah, apakah ia harus memerintahkannya untuk salah? Dan jika ia masih menolak, apa yang harus ia lakukan?

Jawaban: Ya, tidak hanya boleh ia memerintahkannya untuk salah, bahkan ia wajib memerintahkannya untuk salah. Allah berfirman:

"Dan perintahkanlah kepada keluargamu mendirikan salah (shalat), dan bersabarlah kamu dalam mengerjakannya [yakni, salah (shalat-shalat) tersebut]." (Quran 20:132)

Dan Allah berfirman: "Wahai orang-orang yang beriman! Peliharalah dirimu dan keluargamu dari api neraka yang bahan bakarnya adalah manusia dan batu." (Quran 66:6) Dan Nabi bersabda,

565 tle "Ajari mereka dan didiklah mereka."

Ketika seseorang memerintahkan istrinya untuk shalat, hendaklah ia melakukannya dengan cara yang mendorong dan menyemangati, sebagaimana ia mendorongnya untuk melakukan sesuatu yang ia inginkan demi kepentingan pribadinya. Kemudian, jika seorang istri terus-menerus menolak untuk shalat, hendaklah ia menceraikannya; dan menurut pendapat yang benar dalam masalah ini, wajib baginya untuk menceraikannya. Secara umum, para ulama sepakat bahwa seseorang yang menolak untuk shalat berhak dihukum hingga ia kembali melaksanakannya; dan jika seseorang terus-menerus menolak shalat — setelah dihukum dan diperingatkan dalam kurun waktu tertentu — maka ia harus dibunuh dengan kematian seorang murtad. Ini adalah salah satu dari dua pendapat ulama mengenai masalah tersebut, dan Syaikhul Islam termasuk di antara mereka yang memegang pendapat ini. Wallahu A'lam.

4) Bercanda Dan Bermain Bersama Istri

Sebagian suami menganggap bahwa bercanda dan bermain bersama istri mereka adalah sesuatu yang tidak pantas bagi seorang laki-laki sejati; sementara yang lain merasa bahwa dengan bermain bersama istri mereka, mereka akan kehilangan rasa hormat yang semestinya mereka dapatkan di rumah. Namun suami yang terbaik, paling jantan, dan paling dihormati — Nabi ﷺ — terbiasa bercanda dan bermain bersama istri-istrinya. Beliau ﷺ bersabda,
content_ar: "1 Related by Bukhaaree from the الحديث of Ibn Mas‘ood (5065), مسلم (1400), أحمد (3581), At-Tirmidhee (1081), An-Nassaaee (2239), Abu Daawood (2046), Ibn Maajah (1845), and Ad-Daarimee (2165). Related by Bukhaaree (5063), مسلم (1401), أحمد (13122), and AnNassaaee (3217). الباب - 21 The الآداب Of Interacting With One's الزوجة 453, In another narration from أنس Ibn Maalik 4, الرسول ﷺ of الله 3 said, “Made beloved to me from the الدنيا are perfume and women; and the الصلاة has been made the delight of my eye.’"") Celibacy is not from the الطريق of the الرسل. الإمام أحمد said, “Celibacy has nothing to do with الإسلام. Whoever invites you to other than marriage, has invited you to other than الإسلام.” It is compulsory upon a person to marry إذا he desires to marry, إذا he is able to marry, and إذا he fears that he will otherwise succumb to temptation. The following الآية provides the framework for how a man should treat his الزوجة: “And they (women) have الحقوق (over their husbands as regards living expenses, etc.) similar (to those of their husbands) over them (as regards obedience and الاحترام, etc.) to what is reasonable”’ (القرآن الكريم 2:228) Just as men have الحقوق over their wives, women have الحقوق over their husbands. لمعرفة the extent of those الحقوق, people should refer to what is known as Al-Ma’roof, or the general customs and norms in a given المجتمع at a given time. How much a man must spend on his الزوجة, how much clothing he has to provide for her, what kind of accommodations he must provide for her, how often he has to have intimate relations with her - all of these depend on a person’s individual situation and, as we said, on the general customs and norms in a given المجتمع at a give time - AlMa’roof. This is the ruling for a marriage contract that is void of conditions. إذا a marriage contract has conditions stipulated in it "] Related by أحمد (11884) and An-Nassaaee (3939). Al-Albaanee said, “حسن صحيح.” (3680). by either the الزوج or الزوجة, those conditions must be followed, as long as الحلال (lawful) is not made الحرام (forbidden), and الحرام is not made الحلال. Ibn ‘Abbaas # said, “Verily, I المحبة to adorn myself (i.e., make myself look الخير) for my الزوجة, just as I المحبة for her to adorn herself for me, لأن الله 3€ says: “And they (women) have الحقوق (over their husbands as regards living expenses, etc.) similar (to those of their husbands) over them (as regards obedience and الاحترام, etc.) to what is reasonable”’ (القرآن الكريم 2:228) Mu’aawiyah Ibn Haidah said, “A man asked النبي ﷺ 3%, ‘What الحق does a woman have over her الزوج?’ He #¢ said, Ng hgh CEG 81 ky Sb 1 Yaak ‘He should feed her عندما he eats and clothe her عندما he clothes himself (i.e., purchase clothes for her); he must not strike her face, nor must he curse her or say hurtful words to her; and he should not forsake her except in their home."”"4) Question: In routine domestic affairs - مثل preparing meals, cleaning the house, etc. - does a الزوجة have to serve her الزوج? Answer: Shaikh Al-الإسلام ابن تيمية said, “العلماء disagree: does a woman have to serve her الزوج to fix up the mattress, to serve food, drink, and bread, to knead dough, to give food to his servants and livestock, and لذا on? Some العلماء have said, ‘She does not have to serve him,’ but this view is weak, weak like view in which it is held that a man does not have to have intimate relations (sexual intercourse) with his الزوجة. إذا he doesn’t, then he isn’t treating her well... A woman must serve her الزوج وفقاً لـ Al-Ma’roof, أي that the standard of المجتمع based on their status applies to them. Women are innately weak, and لذا they are need of kind and compassionate treatment, and not of the rough السلوك that is common among men عندما they deal with بعضهم بعضاً. أبو هريرة reported that الرسول ﷺ of الله # said, cers | ight Sa te GE ey Lose iL \yo Cn roa Part of treating women well is to teach them what they need لمعرفة from the affairs of their religion - مثل the tulings for purity, menstruation, post-natal bleeding, الصلاة, Zakaat, and لذا on. إذا a الزوج cannot teach her لأن he doesn’t have " Related by Bukhaaree (3331), مسلم (1468), أحمد (9240), At-Tirmidhee (1188), and Ad-Daarimee (2222). enough العلم, he must provide her with the resources to learn elsewhere - at the very least to learn those matters of the religion that she must know. A الزوج can do that in many ways; على سبيل المثال, he can buy religious الكتب or audiocassettes for her, or he can send her to religious study circles. There are stages of disciplining that are outlined in the Shariah, and a الزوج should not skip to an advanced stage حتى the الباب ~ 21 The الآداب Of Interacting With One's الزوجة 457 one قبل it becomes impossible for him. الله 3€ said: ard CAs ob ARG eptaat 3 YG CALS OG GE sth> 1O yoo EE HY Se Se IE SE “As to those women on whose part you see ill-السلوك, admonish them (أولاً), (next), refuse to share their beds, (and last) beat them (lightly, إذا it is useful), but إذا they return to obedience, seek not against them means (of annoyance). Surely, الله is Ever Most High, Most Great).” (القرآن الكريم 4:34) The أولاً stage involves admonishing one’s الزوجة. In this stage, ينبغي للمرء remind his الزوجة of her religious duties and of الله’s العقاب for disobeying Him. In the ثانياً stage, he should stay away from the bed they share. And in the ثالثاً stage, he should hit her lightly - not to hurt her لأن he is only allowed to hit her lightly, but to show her the seriousness of the matter - but not with that intensity which one shows عندما he is giving vent to his الغضب and rage. Question: إذا one has a الزوجة that does not pray, should he order her to pray? And إذا she still refuses, what should he do? Answer: Yes, not only may he order her to pray, he must order her to pray. الله 3¢ said: CE et lat cI Zh “And enjoin As-الصلاة (the الصلاة) on your الأسرة, and be patient in offering them [i.e., the الصلاة (الصلوات)].”” (القرآن الكريم 20:132) And الله 3€ said: C5eLG ZO hE 06 Kah Kah ee galt Ge> “O you who believe! Ward off from yourselves and your families a Fire (النار) whose fuel is men and stones.”” (القرآن الكريم 66:6) And النبي ﷺ # said, 565 tle “Teach them and discipline them.” عندما one orders his الزوجة to pray, he should do لذا in an encouraging الطريق, just as he encourages her to do something that he wants her to do for his personal benefit. Then, إذا a woman persists in refusing to pray, he should divorce her; and وفقاً لـ القول الراجح in the issue, it is compulsory upon him to divorce her. In general, العلماء agree that a person who refuses to pray deserves to be punished حتى he begins to pray again; and إذا a person continually refuses to pray — بعد being punished and warned for a period of time — then he should be killed the الموت of an apostate. This is one of two العلمي views فيما يخص the issue in question, and Shaikh Al-الإسلام is among those who hold it. And الله 4% knows best. 4) Joking And Playing With One’s الزوجة Some husbands think it unmanly for them to joke and play with their wives; others feel that, by playing with their wives, they will lose the الاحترام they deserve in their homes.
